alerts.title: Trump reportedly weighing new strikes on Iran despite 'largely negotiated' deal
alerts.summary: The New York Times reports President Trump is actively weighing options for fresh strikes on Iran even as he publicly claims the Hormuz/ceasefire deal is 'largely negotiated.' Rybar (~95K views) corroborates that US intelligence officials continue to push back against the president, with NYT-staged leaks since May 12 detailing simultaneous US-Israeli strike preparation. Fortune notes Tehran is growing more suspicious of generous ceasefire terms, suggesting both sides anticipate renewed kinetic action.

alerts.title: Khamenei sets red line: enriched uranium must remain inside Iran
alerts.summary: A Reuters exclusive cited in the nuclear feed reports Iranian sources saying Supreme Leader Khamenei has personally ruled out transferring Iran's 60% U-235 stockpile out of the country — a core US demand. Russia's IAEA envoy publicly labeled the US nuclear conditions as NPT violations. With breakout estimated at 1-2 weeks, the position effectively forecloses the central nonproliferation plank of any deal.

alerts.title: Iran says US deal 'not imminent' as Trump tells negotiators 'not to rush'
alerts.summary: BBC reports Iranian officials publicly stating a deal with Washington is not imminent, while a parallel BBC dispatch quotes Trump instructing US negotiators 'not to rush.' The dual messaging — alongside Israeli opposition leader Lapid calling the emerging framework 'bad for the region' (PBS) and a New Yorker piece framing any plausible deal as 'a humiliation' — points to deliberate slowing of the negotiating tempo on both sides.

alerts.title: Israeli strikes on Gaza displacement camps kill women, children, infant
alerts.summary: Al Jazeera, TRT World, Anadolu and Yeni Safak report multiple Israeli strikes today on displaced-Palestinians tent camps in Gaza, killing a woman and child in one incident and three family members including an infant in another. The clustering of four geolocated strikes around 31.27–31.75N / 34.2–34.7E indicates a fresh tempo of operations against displacement sites despite the broader regional focus on Iran negotiations.

alerts.title: FSB claims it foiled maritime sabotage on Belgian-origin gas tanker at Ust-Luga
alerts.summary: Rybar (~114K views) reports the FSB intercepted an alleged sabotage attempt against the gas carrier 'Arrhenius,' which had arrived at Russia's Ust-Luga port from Antwerp, Belgium. Russian authorities say several maritime explosive devices were found in the engine room. The claim — paired with recent Ukrainian strikes on Ust-Luga infrastructure — signals a potential new front of underwater/port sabotage targeting Russia's gas logistics chain, with implications for Baltic shipping risk.

analysis_note: Threat level remains CRITICAL at 9/10: even as Trump publicly markets a 'largely negotiated' Iran deal, NYT reporting and Khamenei's hardening uranium red line indicate both sides are simultaneously preparing for renewed strikes, while parallel maritime-sabotage signaling in the Baltic underscores that the Ukraine theater is also escalating in non-kinetic vectors.
